title: carum alpinum subsp. corydalinum
intro: Carum alpinum subsp. corydalinum, also known as mountain caraway or caraway, is a subspecies of the Carum carvi plant, a member of the parsley family native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a perennial herb with small white flowers and a distinctive, slightly bitter taste, often used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving digestion, and supporting respiratory health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for gastrointestinal issues and respiratory problems. Carum alpinum subsp. corydalinum is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or oil infusion, and may also be used as a spice in culinary dishes.
